description: Redesign the Chrome extension side panel to be a full plugin lifecycle control center. Add an omnisearch bar that searches installed plugins/tools (client-side) and available npm plugins (server-side). Add plugin install, uninstall, and update actions directly in the side panel UI. Add a context menu to each installed plugin card with update and uninstall options. All new UI uses existing retro design system components. Every new and modified component gets Storybook stories.

userStories.title: Add bridge API functions for plugin search, install, remove, and update
userStories.description: As a side panel developer, I want bridge functions that call the existing server-side JSON-RPC methods (plugin.search, plugin.install, plugin.remove, plugin.updateFromRegistry) so that the UI can manage plugin lifecycle without new server changes.
acceptanceCriteria: bridge.ts exports searchPlugins(query: string) that calls plugin.search and returns { results: PluginSearchResult[] }
bridge.ts exports installPlugin(name: string) that calls plugin.install and returns { ok: true, plugin: { name, displayName, version, toolCount } }
bridge.ts exports removePlugin(name: string) that calls plugin.remove and returns { ok: true }
bridge.ts exports updatePlugin(name: string) that calls plugin.updateFromRegistry and returns { ok: true, plugin: { name, displayName, version, toolCount } }
A PluginSearchResult type is defined in bridge.ts with fields: name, description, version, author, isOfficial
All four functions use the existing sendRequest pattern with proper typing
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: The server already handles all four JSON-RPC methods (see platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts lines 407-430 and platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts). The side panel just needs to call them via the existing sendRequest function in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts. Follow the exact pattern of fetchConfigState, setToolEnabled, setAllToolsEnabled — those use sendRequest and cast the return type. The PluginSearchResult type mirrors the server-side interface in platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.ts:31-37. Also export a PluginInstallResult type with { ok: true, plugin: { name: string, displayName: string, version: string, toolCount: number } }. Add a matchesPlugin helper (similar to existing matchesTool) that checks if a PluginState's displayName, name, or tool names/descriptions match a filter string.

userStories.title: Create NpmPluginCard component for npm search results
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see available npm plugins as retro-styled cards in search results so that I can browse and install plugins directly from the side panel.
acceptanceCriteria: NpmPluginCard.tsx exists at platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/NpmPluginCard.tsx
Renders plugin name with PluginIcon (letter avatar, using existing PluginIcon component with tabState='closed')
Shows version as a Badge (retro/Badge.tsx, variant='default', size='sm')
Shows author text in text-muted-foreground font-mono text-xs
Shows an official badge (Badge with bg-primary text) when isOfficial is true
Shows description text truncated to 2 lines with text-muted-foreground text-xs
Has an Install button (retro/Button.tsx, size='sm', variant='default') on the right side of the header
Install button shows Loader (retro/Loader.tsx, size='sm') when installing prop is true
Install button is disabled when installing prop is true
Shows inline Alert (retro/Alert.tsx, status='error') when error prop is set
Card follows retro container pattern: rounded border-2 border-border bg-card shadow-md
Card has hover effect: transition-all hover:shadow-sm
NpmPluginCard.stories.tsx exists with stories: Default, Official, Installing, WithError, LongDescription, AllStates composite
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: Component props: { plugin: PluginSearchResult, installing: boolean, error: string | null, onInstall: () => void }. The card layout should be compact for the narrow side panel (~350px). Layout: a single row header with [PluginIcon(size=28)] [name + version badge] [Install button], then a second row with description text, then optional error Alert. Use the existing PluginIcon component from platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginIcon.tsx — pass tabState='closed' since these aren't installed yet. The PluginIcon handles letter avatars with deterministic colors automatically. For the 'official' indicator, use the existing Badge component with className='bg-primary/20 text-primary-foreground' and text 'OFFICIAL'. Follow the existing story pattern in PluginCard.stories.tsx — use Meta with title 'Components/NpmPluginCard', wrap in a w-80 decorator.

userStories.title: Create SearchResults component for omnisearch results view
userStories.description: As a user, I want search results to show both matching installed plugins/tools and available npm plugins in clearly separated sections so that I can find and manage everything from one place.
acceptanceCriteria: SearchResults.tsx exists at platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/SearchResults.tsx
Has two sections: 'INSTALLED' and 'AVAILABLE' with section headers styled as font-head text-muted-foreground text-[10px] uppercase tracking-widest mb-1.5
INSTALLED section shows filtered installed plugins using existing PluginCard accordion pattern (reuses PluginList with toolFilter)
AVAILABLE section shows NpmPluginCard components for each npm search result
AVAILABLE section is hidden when npm search is loading (shows Loader instead, centered)
AVAILABLE section is hidden when search query is empty
Shows 'No results' message (text-muted-foreground text-sm text-center py-8) when both sections are empty
Filters out npm results that match already-installed plugin names (prevents showing 'Install' for already installed plugins)
SearchResults.stories.tsx exists with stories: InstalledOnly, NpmOnly, Both, NoResults, NpmLoading, InstallingPlugin, AllStates composite
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: Component props: { plugins: PluginState[], failedPlugins: FailedPluginState[], activeTools: Set<string>, setPlugins: Dispatch<SetStateAction<PluginState[]>>, toolFilter: string, npmResults: PluginSearchResult[], npmSearching: boolean, installingPlugins: Set<string>, onInstall: (name: string) => void, installErrors: Map<string, string> }. The INSTALLED section reuses the existing PluginList component directly, passing through the toolFilter. The AVAILABLE section renders NpmPluginCard for each result whose name is NOT in the installed plugins set. Use the existing matchesPlugin helper from bridge.ts (added in US-001) to filter installed plugins by the search query — show plugins where the name, displayName, or any tool name/description matches. For stories, create mock PluginSearchResult arrays and reuse the mockPlugin patterns from SidePanel.stories.tsx. The component should be a scrollable container that replaces the normal PluginList view when the search query is non-empty.

userStories.title: Create PluginMenu context menu for installed plugin cards
userStories.description: As a user, I want a context menu on each installed plugin card with Update and Uninstall options so that I can manage plugin lifecycle directly from the side panel.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginMenu.tsx exists at platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginMenu.tsx
Uses the existing retro/Menu.tsx (Radix DropdownMenu) component — not a new component
Trigger is a MoreHorizontal (lucide) icon button, 24x24, styled as a ghost button (no border/shadow, hover:bg-muted/50)
Menu has 'Update' item that is only visible when plugin.update is truthy
Update item shows the available version (e.g., 'Update to v0.2.0') with text-foreground
Menu has a separator between Update and Uninstall when both are visible
Menu has 'Uninstall' item with text-destructive color
Clicking Uninstall changes the item text to 'Confirm?' for 3 seconds, then reverts to 'Uninstall'
Clicking 'Confirm?' triggers the onRemove callback
The menu does NOT appear for plugins with source='local' (local dev plugins cannot be uninstalled from UI)
PluginMenu.stories.tsx exists with stories: Default, WithUpdate, LocalPlugin (hidden), ConfirmState, AllStates composite
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: Component props: { plugin: PluginState, onUpdate: () => void, onRemove: () => void, updating: boolean, removing: boolean }. Use the existing Menu component from platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Menu.tsx. For the confirmation UX: use a useState(false) for confirmPending, and a useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>> for the 3-second revert timer. When 'Uninstall' is clicked, set confirmPending=true and start a 3s timer. If 'Confirm?' is clicked, call onRemove(). If the timer fires, reset confirmPending=false. Clean up the timer on unmount. The trigger button must use onClick with e.stopPropagation() so it doesn't toggle the accordion. Show Loader (size='sm') next to Update text when updating=true, and next to Uninstall when removing=true. For the MoreHorizontal icon: import { MoreHorizontal } from 'lucide-react'. The button container needs the same isolation from accordion as the Switch in PluginCard.tsx — wrap in a div with onClick/onKeyDown stopPropagation and role='presentation'. For Menu.Item separator, add a div with className='h-px bg-border mx-1 my-1' between items (the existing Menu component does not have a Separator sub-component). For stories, follow the Menu.stories.tsx pattern.

userStories.title: Integrate PluginMenu into PluginCard header
userStories.description: As a user, I want the context menu (three-dot icon) to appear in each installed plugin card header between the chevron and the master switch so that I can access Update and Uninstall actions.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginCard.tsx renders PluginMenu between the chevron and the Switch in the accordion header
PluginMenu is hidden for local plugins (source='local')
PluginCard accepts onUpdate and onRemove callback props
PluginCard accepts updatingPlugin and removingPlugin boolean props
The PluginMenu trigger (MoreHorizontal icon) is visually aligned with the chevron and switch
When removing, the entire card has opacity-60 and pointer-events-none styling
PluginCard.stories.tsx updated with new stories: WithMenu, WithMenuAndUpdate, RemovingState
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx, the accordion header layout is: [AccordionPrimitive.Trigger with icon+name+badges+chevron] [div with Switch]. Insert a new div between them for PluginMenu, with the same isolation pattern (onClick/onKeyDown stopPropagation, role='presentation'). The PluginMenu trigger should be a simple button with no visible border — just the MoreHorizontal icon that appears on hover or when menu is open. Keep the existing PluginCard props, add: onUpdate?: () => void, onRemove?: () => void, updatingPlugin?: boolean, removingPlugin?: boolean. When removingPlugin is true, wrap the entire Accordion.Item content in a div with 'opacity-60 pointer-events-none transition-opacity'. The PluginMenu should only render when source !== 'local' — local/dev plugins are managed via the filesystem, not npm. Update the existing stories to pass the new props, and add stories showing the menu states.

userStories.title: Upgrade search bar to omnisearch and rewire App.tsx state management
userStories.description: As a user, I want the search bar to always appear when connected (not just when >5 tools), search across installed plugins/tools instantly, and trigger debounced npm search so that I can discover and manage all plugins from one place.
acceptanceCriteria: Search bar is always visible when connected (removes the totalTools > 5 gate)
Search placeholder text changed from 'Filter tools...' to 'Search plugins and tools...'
toolFilter state renamed to searchQuery in App.tsx
Typing triggers client-side filtering of installed plugins/tools immediately
Typing triggers debounced (400ms) npm search via bridge.searchPlugins
App.tsx has new state: npmResults (PluginSearchResult[]), npmSearching (boolean), installingPlugins (Set<string>), removingPlugins (Set<string>), installErrors (Map<string, string>)
When searchQuery is non-empty, main content shows SearchResults component instead of PluginList
When searchQuery is empty, main content shows PluginList as before
Install handler calls bridge.installPlugin, manages installingPlugins set, clears search on success
Remove handler calls bridge.removePlugin, manages removingPlugins set
Update handler calls bridge.updatePlugin
SidePanel.stories.tsx updated with new stories: OmnisearchEmpty, OmnisearchWithResults, OmnisearchNpmLoading, OmnisearchInstalling
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
This is the integration story that wires everything together in App.tsx. Key changes:

1. Remove the `const showSearchBar = connected && !loading && totalTools > 5` gate. Replace with `const showSearchBar = connected && !loading`.

2. Rename toolFilter to searchQuery throughout.

3. Add a useRef for the npm search debounce timer. On searchQuery change, clear the timer, set a new 400ms timeout that calls searchPlugins(searchQuery). On empty query, clear npmResults.

4. Add state: npmResults, npmSearching, installingPlugins (Set<string>), removingPlugins (Set<string>), installErrors (Map<string, string>).

5. Add handleInstall(name: string): set installingPlugins + name, call installPlugin(name), on success remove from installingPlugins and clear installErrors for name and clear searchQuery, on error set installErrors.set(name, message) and remove from installingPlugins.

6. Add handleRemove(pluginName: string): set removingPlugins + name, call removePlugin(name), on success remove from removingPlugins (plugins.changed notification will refresh the list), on error remove from removingPlugins and show error.

7. Add handleUpdate(pluginName: string): call updatePlugin(name). On success, plugins.changed will refresh.

8. In the render, when searchQuery is non-empty, show <SearchResults> instead of <PluginList>. Pass all the new state and handlers.

9. Pass onUpdate and onRemove through to PluginList and then to PluginCard.

10. Update PluginList.tsx to accept and pass through onUpdate, onRemove, updatingPlugin, removingPlugins props.

This is opus-level because it touches App.tsx (the root state manager), PluginList, PluginCard, and SearchResults with state threading across 4 components. The debounce logic, optimistic UI state management for install/remove, and the conditional rendering switch between PluginList and SearchResults need careful coordination.

The NoPluginsState component (line 70-79) renders an Empty card with title 'No Plugins Installed', separator, description, and code block. Change the Empty.Description text to 'Search for plugins above or install via CLI:' and keep the code block showing 'opentabs plugin'. This guides users to the omnisearch bar (which is now always visible when connected) while keeping the CLI fallback.

userStories.notes: In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Menu.tsx, add a MenuSeparator component. Follow the exact pattern of MenuItem — it's a React.forwardRef wrapping DropdownMenuPrimitive.Separator. The class should be 'h-px bg-border -mx-1 my-1' (this matches the ContextMenu.Separator pattern from the RetroUI upstream repo at /tmp/RetroUI/components/retroui/ContextMenu.tsx line 212). Add it to the MenuObject assignment as Separator: MenuSeparator. Import DropdownMenuPrimitive.Separator is already available since the file imports * as DropdownMenuPrimitive. Update Menu.stories.tsx to add a 'WithSeparator' story showing items with a separator between groups.

userStories.title: Full assembled side panel Storybook stories for all new states

acceptanceCriteria: SidePanel.stories.tsx has new stories: OmnisearchInstalledResults, OmnisearchNpmResults, OmnisearchMixed, OmnisearchInstalling, OmnisearchNoResults, PluginWithContextMenu, PluginRemoving, PluginUpdating
Each story uses the SidePanelShell pattern from existing stories

OmnisearchMixed story shows both installed matches and npm results simultaneously
OmnisearchInstalling story shows one npm card in installing state
PluginRemoving story shows one installed card in removing state (opacity-60)
All existing stories still work unchanged

userStories.notes: Update platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/SidePanel.stories.tsx. Follow the exact existing pattern — each story is a render function component with useState for plugins. The SidePanelShell wrapper already exists. For omnisearch stories, create a new OmnisearchShell that includes SearchResults instead of PluginList when a searchQuery is provided. Mock npm results using PluginSearchResult arrays (e.g., { name: '@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-notion', description: 'OpenTabs plugin for Notion', version: '1.0.0', author: 'opentabs-dev', isOfficial: true }). For the PluginRemoving story, pass removingPlugins={new Set(['slack'])} to show the opacity effect. For PluginUpdating, show a plugin card with update available and updating=true. Reuse the existing mock helpers (mockPlugin, githubPlugin, etc.) and add new ones for npm results.
